errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.config: Backend not configured. Set the Supabase environment variables.

hero.baseline: From detection to decision. In seconds. Even jammed.
hero.subtitle: The tactical web platform that detects drones with on-device AI, maps jamming zones and keeps communications alive - no install, no infrastructure.
hero.builtAt: Built in 48h - European Defense Tech Hackathon · Paris · June 2026
hero.voiceTagline: We translate voice onto a map for real-time positional tracking.

problem.title: The sky has changed. The tools haven't.
problem.c1Body: Low-cost drones overwhelm defenses designed for a different era.
problem.c2Body: Jamming cuts communications exactly when coordination matters most.
problem.c3Body: Detections, reports and orders live in different tools - decisions arrive late.

solution.title: One URL. Four capabilities.
solution.sentinelBody: AI runs in your browser. Detection in under 3 seconds, even without network.
solution.mapBody: Every reported jamming zone appears for the whole team in under 2 seconds - and three reports are enough to localize the emitter.
solution.operationBody: The entire tactical picture on a single screen.
solution.ghostBody: Cut the network. Your messages wait, then synchronize. In order, without duplicates.

challenge.badge: EDTH Paris 2026 - Durandal challenge: collaborative jammer detection and localization
challenge.title: They jam. We triangulate.
challenge.body: The challenge: detect, classify and geolocate hostile RF interference with distributed sensors. AIGLONIX's distributed sensors are the operators themselves - every phone becomes a node of the electronic-warfare picture.
challenge.featureBody: Three reports of the same band are enough: AIGLONIX estimates the emitter's position by strength-weighted triangulation and shares the fix with the whole team in under 2 seconds, with an honest uncertainty radius. The jamming zone stops being something you endure - it becomes a target you can act on.
challenge.s1: Detect - every report is a bearing; the operators are the distributed sensor network.
challenge.s2: Classify - by frequency band (2.4 GHz, 5.8 GHz, GPS L1, 900 MHz), with an automatic countermeasure: the frequency-switch recommendation.
challenge.s3: Operate denied - offline-first by design: on-device AI, outbox communications, cached tactical picture.

how.title: How it works
how.s1Body: Point your phone - on-device AI identifies the threat.
how.s2Body: The detection propagates to the whole team in real time.
how.s3Body: The unified tactical view turns signals into decisions.

proof.title: Not a mockup. Production.
proof.note: Everything you see runs in production, right now.

security.title: Built for hostile environments
security.p1Body: Forced HTTPS, armored sessions - httpOnly cookies, no token ever exposed.
security.p2Body: One official URL, displayed here. AIGLONIX never asks for your credentials.
security.p3Body: Accounts protected against bots and breached passwords (Turnstile + HaveIBeenPwned check).
security.p4Body: GPS metadata in your photos is destroyed before anything is sent.
security.p6Body: Every action is attributed and timestamped, immutably.

why.title: Why AIGLONIX
why.p1: Zero install - one URL, operational in 30 seconds.
why.p2: On-device AI - works in jammed or disconnected areas.
why.p3: Open architecture - Next.js + Supabase, auditable, extensible.

roadmap.title: Beyond the hackathon
roadmap.m1: Specialized AI model - Shahed / FPV recognition

finalCta.title: Ready to enter the command post?

footer.edth: European Defense Tech Hackathon · Paris · June 2026
